# ============================================
# Docker - Build and Push Pipeline
# ============================================
# PURPOSE: Builds a Docker image of our API
# and pushes it to Azure Container Registry
# Runs on every push to master AFTER CI passes
# ============================================
# WHY DOCKER?
# Packages our app + .NET runtime + config into
# one portable "container" that runs anywhere
# "Works on my machine" problem = SOLVED forever
# ============================================

name: Docker - Build and Push  # Shows in GitHub Actions tab

# TRIGGERS - when does this pipeline run?
on:
  push:                        # Run when code is pushed
    branches:
      - master                 # Only on master branch
                               # No PR trigger - we only
                               # push images from master

# JOBS
jobs:
  docker-build-push:           # Job name
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest     # MUST be Linux!
                               # Our Docker image is Linux-based
                               # windows-latest would fail here
                               # (learned this the hard way! 😄)

    steps:
      # Step 1 - Download code from GitHub onto build server
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      # Step 2 - Authenticate with Azure Container Registry
      # docker/login-action = pre-built action for Docker login
      # registry = where we're logging into (our ACR)
      # username/password = from GitHub Secrets (never exposed in logs)
      # ${{ secrets.X }} = reads from GitHub repository secrets
      - name: Log in to Azure Container Registry
        uses: docker/login-action@v3
        with:
          registry: ${{ secrets.REGISTRY_LOGIN_SERVER }}
          username: ${{ secrets.REGISTRY_USERNAME }}
          password: ${{ secrets.REGISTRY_PASSWORD }}

      # Step 3 - Build Docker image from our Dockerfile
      # docker build = reads Dockerfile and creates image
      # -t = tag (name) for the image
      # Format: registry/imagename:tag
      # latest = always points to most recent build
      # ./SmartJobTracker.API = folder containing Dockerfile
      - name: Build Docker image
        run: docker build -t ${{ secrets.REGISTRY_LOGIN_SERVER }}/smartjobtracker-api:latest ./SmartJobTracker.API

      # Step 4 - Push image to Azure Container Registry
      # docker push = upload image to registry
      # Now Azure Container Apps can pull and run it
      # Anyone with registry access can pull this image
      - name: Push Docker image to registry
        run: docker push ${{ secrets.REGISTRY_LOGIN_SERVER }}/smartjobtracker-api:latest
